latest.content:
14 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1690M
Physiomics PLC
14 July 2026
14 July 2026
Physiomics plc
("Physiomics" or the "Company")
Director Dealing
Physiomics plc (AIM: PYC), a leading mathematical modelling, data science and biostatistics company supporting the development of new therapeutics and personalised medicine solutions, announces that Ian Bagnall, Director of the Company, has acquired, via market purchases, 1,111,111 new ordinary shares of 0.4p in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") at a price of 0.45p per Ordinary Share.
Accordingly, Mr Bagnall's total holding in the Company will be 16,111,111 Ordinary Shares, representing approximately 3.55% of the Company's issued share capital.
ENDS
For more information about Physiomics and its services, please visit
www.physiomics.co.uk
.
Enquiries:
Physiomics plc
Mike Whitlow
Email: info@physiomics.co.uk
Strand Hanson Ltd (NOMAD and Broker)
James Dance & James Bellman
Tel: +44 (0) 20 7409 3494
About Physiomics
Physiomics plc combines expertise across Modelling & Simulation, Biostatistics, Data Science and Bioinformatics, together with deep biology expertise, to help biotech and pharma companies streamline their drug development journeys. Our approach is to help derive insight from all relevant and often disparate data in order to de-risk decision making and optimise research design across discovery, pre-clinical and clinical studies. Through use of cutting- edge computational tools, bespoke models and our proprietary Virtual Tumour technology, the Physiomics team has informed the development of over 140 commercial projects, with over 125 targets and drugs modelled. Clients include Merck KGaA, Astellas, Bicycle Therapeutics, Numab Therapeutics & CRUK.

latest.title: Physiomics Awarded Multiple New Contracts
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4038L
Physiomics PLC
08 July 2026
8 July
2026
Physiomics plc
("Physiomics" or the "Company")
Physiomics Awarded Multiple New Contracts
Physiomics plc (AIM: PYC), a leading mathematical modelling, data science and biostatistics company supporting the development of new therapeutics and personalised medicine solutions, is pleased to announce that it has secured multiple contract awards with a combined estimated value in excess of £200,000.
The awards comprise a combination of follow-on engagements within the Company's Biometrics service line and a new contract within its Modelling & Simulation business. The Biometrics awards include two extensions to existing clinical development engagements: one with a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on immunology and one with a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on infectious disease.
In addition, the Company has secured a new Modelling & Simulation contract with a Boston-based biotechnology company developing novel immunotherapies for cancer.
The projects will utilise the Company's expertise across biostatistics, modelling and simulation to support key stages of drug development, including data analysis, development programme support and model-informed development. The Board believes these awards further demonstrate the continued development of Physiomics' diversified service offering, the strength of its client relationships and its ability to support increasingly complex projects across both existing and new clients.
Jesse Thissen, Chief Operating Officer of Physiomics, commented:
"We are pleased to have secured these awards, which reflect the continued progress of our Biometrics service line alongside our established Modelling & Simulation capabilities. Since opening our Biometrics department, we have focused on building the processes and technical infrastructure required to support more complex client engagements for both Biometrics and MIDD. The mix of follow-on and new work across Biometrics and Modelling & Simulation demonstrates the increasing breadth of our offering and our ability to provide quantitative support to biotech clients across multiple stages of development."
ENDS
For more information about Physiomics and its services, please visit
www.physiomics.co.uk
.

Physiomics plc
Mike Whitlow

Our approach is to help derive insight from all relevant and often disparate data in order to de-risk decision making and optimise research design across discovery, pre-clinical and clinical studies. Through use of cutting- edge computational tools, bespoke models and our proprietary Virtual Tumour technology, the Physiomics team has informed the development of over 140 commercial projects, with over 125 targets and drugs modelled. Clients include Merck KGaA, Astellas, Bicycle Therapeutics, Numab Therapeutics & CRUK.

latest.title: Appointment of Chief Operating Officer
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4810K
Physiomics PLC
01 July 2026
1 July
2026
Physiomics plc
("Physiomics" or the "Company")
Appointment of Chief Operating Officer
Physiomics plc (AIM: PYC), a leading mathematical modelling, data science and biostatistics company supporting the development of new therapeutics and personalised medicine solutions, is pleased to announce the promotion of Jesse Thissen to the position of Chief Operating Officer (COO) with immediate effect.
Jesse has served as Head of Biometrics and has played an instrumental role in strengthening the Company's operational capabilities, technical leadership and project delivery. During his tenure, he has led a number of strategic initiatives designed to improve operational efficiency, expand the Company's service offering and enhance its ability to execute increasingly complex and larger-scale client engagements.
In his new role as Chief Operating Officer, Jesse will be responsible for overseeing the Company's day-to-day operations, supporting continued excellence in project delivery while driving operational scalability and supporting the Company's next phase of commercial growth.
Mike Whitlow, Executive Director of Physiomics, commented:
"Jesse's promotion to Chief Operating Officer reflects the exceptional leadership, innovation and ambition he has demonstrated throughout his time at Physiomics. He has been instrumental in delivering a number of important operational improvements which we believe will strengthen the Company for many years to come.
"These changes are enhancing the way we operate and position Physiomics to pursue larger and more strategically significant contracts, broaden our service offering and further improve the speed, quality and scalability of our delivery. As demand for our services continues to grow, Jesse's leadership will be central to executing our strategy and creating long-term value for shareholders.
"On behalf of the Board, I congratulate Jesse on his appointment and look forward to working closely with him as we continue to build a stronger, more efficient and increasingly scalable business."
Jesse Thissen, Chief Operating Officer, commented:
"I am delighted to accept the role of Chief Operating Officer at such an important stage in Physiomics' development. Over the past year, we have made strong progress in establishing Biometrics as a revenue-generating service line, while also laying the foundations for scalable, high-quality delivery across the business. Physiomics has a talented team with deep scientific and technical expertise, and I look forward to supporting the Company's next phase of growth by strengthening operational execution, expanding our service offering and ensuring we continue to deliver excellent work to our clients."
ENDS
For more information about Physiomics and its services, please visit
www.physiomics.co.uk
.

latest.title: Proposed Issue of Equity and Grant of Options
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 2632K
Physiomics PLC
30 June 2026
30 June 2026
Physiomics plc
("Physiomics" or the "Company")
Proposed Issue of Equity and Grant of Options
Physiomics plc (AIM: PYC), a leading mathematical modelling, data science and biostatistics company supporting the development of new therapeutics and personalised medicine solutions, announces the conditional issue of new ordinary shares of 0.40 pence each in Physiomics (the "Ordinary Shares") in respect of the board of directors of Physiomics' (the "Board" or the "Directors") remuneration policy as announced on 8 May 2026, whereby each Director is remunerated partially through the issue of new Ordinary Shares.
Nick Tulloch, Chairman, will receive 4,000,000 new Ordinary Shares, representing remuneration of £20,000. Mike Whitlow, Executive Director, and Ian Bagnall, Finance Director, will each receive 5,000,000 new Ordinary Shares, representing remuneration of £25,000.
All of the new Ordinary Shares are to be issued at a price of 0.50 pence per new Ordinary Share, being the closing price on 28 April 2026, the day before the Directors joined the Board. It is intended that awards of
Ordinary Shares to Directors will be made once in each financial year in respect of part of their remuneration.
As the Company currently has no headroom to issue new Ordinary Shares, the above share awards to the Directors will be made following the Company's next annual general meeting ("AGM") at which resolutions will be proposed to grant authorities for the issue of the new Ordinary Shares and the Options described below.
Physiomics further
announces the grant of options ("Options") to Directors for a total of 20,139,858 Ordinary Shares.
Each Option provides the holder with the right to receive one new Ordinary Share on its exercise. 10,909,089 of the Options were granted with an exercise price of 0.55 pence per new Ordinary Share and 9,230,769 of the Options were granted with an exercise price of 0.65 pence per new Ordinary Share, representing a premium of approximately 15 per cent. and approximately 35 per cent. respectively to the mid-market closing price of 0.48 pence per Ordinary Share on 26 June 2026.
The Options in aggregate will represent 4.3 per cent of the Company's share capital,
ceteris paribus
, as enlarged by the issue of the New Ordinary Shares above. The Options will expire on the fifth anniversary of the date of grant if not exercised.
These option awards reflect the Board's policy to conserve the Company's cash resources and accept a significant part of their remuneration as equity in the Company. The Board further considers that these awards of Options offer significant incentive and alignment with shareholders as a whole in relation to Physiomics' future success, for which the Board believes the Company is well positioned, supported by its robust operational progress and strategic developmental activities.
The Options form part of a new share option scheme and the Company intends to consider option awards on an annual basis in the future. Furthermore, it is the Company's intention to grant further options on the same terms to Physiomics employees.
The following awards have been made:

company_30s.what_they_do: Physiomics delivers advanced translational modelling and contract research services that help pharmaceutical companies speed up drug development by simulating disease biology and predicting clinical outcomes.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global biotech sector is pushing for faster, cheaper pipelines. Physiomics’ modelling tools cut development time and reduce trial costs, giving it a niche in a market that is expanding as more companies outsource early‑stage research.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in a growth phase, securing several new contracts and bringing in fresh leadership. Cash burn is ongoing, but the recent influx of contracts suggests a shift toward revenue generation while the company remains on a tight runway.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show Physiomics winning multiple new contracts, appointing a new COO, and updating its strategy to focus on larger, higher‑value projects. Board changes and a proposed equity issue signal the company is actively seeking capital and governance support.

These events cluster around three themes: (1) a steady stream of contract wins that hint at a growing pipeline; (2) leadership reshuffles aimed at tightening execution; and (3) strategic moves to raise funds and stabilize cash flow.
Together they paint a picture of a company that is trying to turn a lean, contract‑heavy model into a more sustainable, revenue‑generating operation.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter highlights the new contracts and fresh COO as signs that the business is gaining traction and that the market will reward the upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices point to the company’s thin runway, the risk of over‑valuation, and uncertainty around whether the new contracts will translate into real cash flow.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Overall sentiment remains mixed; a few bullish posts about contract wins are offset by bearish concerns over funding and execution risk.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, so any meaningful news could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of the first deliverable from the new contracts, which would validate the company’s value proposition.
A funding round or equity issuance that boosts the balance sheet and removes immediate cash‑burn pressure.
A breakout above the 12‑week moving average accompanied by a volume spike, signalling that the market has started to price in the new upside.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume increase of 2‑3 times the average, a price break above the 12‑week MA, or a press release detailing funding or a major partnership. These are the signals that will confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early post‑crash phase; the compression suggests a potential breakout is on the horizon. Confirmation will come from volume and a clear move above short‑term support.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Deep crashes historically return 160‑200% on average. If the price rebounds to halfway between the recent low and the 12‑week high, the upside is several times the initial outlay. The contract pipeline and leadership changes support a mean‑reversion narrative.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Cash burn remains a risk; if the company cannot deliver on its contracts or secure funding, the price could stay flat or slide further. The worst‑case is a continued decline that erodes the already thin runway.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price because the stock is near a bottom. Potential gain could be 5‑to‑6 times the stake if the mean‑reversion plays out. The asymmetry is highly favourable for a contrarian approach.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed volume spike and a price break above the 12‑week MA. Size 10‑15% of the allocated capital, set a stop 5% below the recent low to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a funding announcement, contract milestone, or a clear volume‑backed breakout occurs. Only then consider a small position to avoid premature exposure.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks identified; the setup remains fundamentally sound.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash, a pipeline of new contracts, and fresh leadership create a high‑reward mean‑reversion play that is primed for a breakout once volume confirms the move.
